title: Templetonia retusa
intro: Templetonia retusa, commonly known as the 'Pinecone Bush' or 'Bottlebrush Bush', is a small shrub native to Western Australia, characterized by its unique, bottlebrush-like flowers and attractive foliage. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in soothing skin irritations and wound healing. Bioactive constituents of Templetonia retusa include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional Western Australian medicine, the plant is often used to prepare a tea or infusion, and its extracts are also incorporated into topical creams and salves for skin care applications.
